perturbation.original_text: 5. Custom er and Minnesota Power hereby acknowledge that unauthorized disclosure or use of the Inform ation m ay result in irreparable harm to Minnesota Power. Accordingly, Custom er agrees that Minnesota Power shall ha ve the right to obtain a tem porary restraining order enjoining disclosure or use of all Inform ation subject to this Agreem ent. Custom er agrees not to contest the granting of such tem porary restraining order. Such order shall becom e perm anent upon adequate showing of irreparable inju ry and ruling by a court of law. The Parties may participate in any contest relating to a perm anent injunction.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Custom er and Minnesota Power hereby acknowledge that unauthorized disclosure or use of the Inform ation m ay result in irreparable harm to Minnesota Power. Accordingly, Custom er agrees that Minnesota Power shall ha ve the right to obtain a tem porary restraining order enjoining disclosure or use of all Inform ation subject to this Agreem ent. Custom er reserves the right to contest the granting of such tem porary restraining order. Such order shall becom e perm anent upon adequate showing of irreparable inju ry and ruling by a court of law. The Parties may participate in any contest relating to a perm anent injunction.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original text by stating that the 'Customer reserves the right to contest the granting of such temporary restraining order', whereas previously the 'Customer agrees not to contest'. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the Customer's ability to challenge a restraining order, leading to uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Custom er and Minnesota Power hereby acknowledge that unauthorized disclosure or use of the Inform ation m ay result in irreparable harm to Minnesota Power. Accordingly, Custom er agrees that Minnesota Power shall ha ve the right to obtain a tem porary restraining order enjoining disclosure or use of all Inform ation subject to this Agreem ent. Custom er reserves the right to contest the granting of such tem porary restraining order. Such order shall becom e perm anent upon adequate showing of irreparable inju ry and ruling by a court of law. The Parties may participate in any contest relating to a perm anent injunction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states 'Customer reserves the right to contest the granting of such temporary restraining order,' directly contradicting the original text's 'Customer agrees not to contest the granting of such temporary restraining order.' The contradicted text is located in the same section as the changed text. The Perturbation Explanation accurately captures this in-text contradiction. The modified text creates a clear conflict, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 8. In further consideration of Minnesota Power disclosing its Information hereunder, Customer agrees that it will not use the Informa tion for any third-party’s benefit or in providing services to third parties (which shall specifically include any wholesale or retail customer served by Minnesota Power).
perturbation.changed_text: 8. In further consideration of Minnesota Power disclosing its Information hereunder, Customer agrees that it m ay use the Informa tion for any third-party’s benefit or in providing services to third parties, as long as those third parties are not competitors of Minnesota Power (which shall specifically include any wholesale or retail customer served by Minnesota Power).
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause by now allowing the customer to use the information for a third-party’s benefit. This creates an in-text contradiction and legal ambiguity because it changes the original intention of preventing the customer from using the information for third-party benefit, now allowing that with the exclusion to competitors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In further consideration of Minnesota Power disclosing its Information hereunder, Customer agrees that it m ay use the Informa tion for any third-party’s benefit or in providing services to third parties, as long as those third parties are not competitors of Minnesota Power (which shall specifically include any wholesale or retail customer served by Minnesota Power).
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the customer 'will not use' the information for any third-party's benefit. The changed text allows the customer to use the information for a third-party's benefit, as long as those third parties are not competitors. This creates a direct conflict because it reverses the original restriction, thus creating an in-text contradiction as it directly opposes the original restriction.
